summary: Iteration 13 (frontend-only): verified the mandatory OnboardingGate blocking modal end-to-end. Signed up a fresh nameless user via Firebase REST + /api/auth/sync, then logged in through the UI. All 13 acceptance criteria passed: gate visible on every authed route (/feed, /discovery, /profile, /messages), input auto-focused, ESC + overlay click + no close button cannot dismiss, Save button disabled when input <2 chars, too-short name shows 'Please use at least 2 characters.' inline error (data-testid=onboarding-error), valid name calls PUT /api/users/me and modal closes immediately (profile.name patched optimistically), gate stays gone on subsequent navigation and after full page reload. Backend untouched this iteration (regression already green in iter12).
critical_code_review_comments: OnboardingGate.jsx correctly omits all dismissal paths (no overlay onClick, no ESC handler, no close button) — exactly the contract the iteration calls for.
patchProfile is called with the server's returned name (res.data.name), so a server-side trimming/normalization stays in sync — good.

Minor nit (non-blocking): when error is set from a previous attempt, it persists until next submit; that's actually preferred UX since the user can still see why their last attempt was rejected. No change needed.
needsName condition uses authReady && isAuthenticated && profile && !(profile.name && profile.name.trim()) — covers null, undefined, empty string, and whitespace-only. Robust.
test_credentials: Fresh user created via Firebase Identity Toolkit REST signup (TEST_i13_<rand>@example.com / demo1234), synced to backend with no name, then deleted post-test. Pattern: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration12.py.
seed_data_creation: One throwaway Firebase + backend user (TEST_i13_e637ace2@example.com) created at test start, deleted at end — no leftover state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: OnboardingGate is mounted in App.jsx between EmailVerificationBanner and Routes. Triggered only when authReady && isAuthenticated && profile && !profile.name. To reproduce the gate condition on demand, use the Firebase REST signUp + POST /api/auth/sync pattern from test_iteration12.py and DO NOT call PUT /api/users/me with a name. Then sign in via the Login UI. The data-testids in scope: onboarding-gate (wrapping div), onboarding-name-input (input), onboarding-save-btn (submit), onboarding-error (inline error). The SignUp form already supplies a name during /signup so the gate never fires for users who came through SignUp — it only fires for users who got into the backend with no name (e.g., via API path or existing production users).
